spec_match.rationale: Runs real encode/decode probes for VP8, H.264, and AV1 when supported, reads decoded VideoFrame.rotation and VideoFrame.flip, and avoids pre-claiming universal bitstream persistence.

interactive.rationale: Rotation and boolean flip controls run the multi-codec probe suite and update per-codec cards plus the event log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede and table now state that codec/backend paths can preserve, normalize, or drop metadata and ask the browser to report the live result.

edge_cases.rationale: Unsupported VideoEncoder/VideoDecoder or codec profiles are reported as skipped; supported codecs report the in/out metadata comparison.

references.rationale: Includes the WebCodecs spec and ChromeStatus entry 5128855343595520.

design_system.rationale: Uses project spacing, typography, borders, and badge classes.

summary: Verified in Chrome 148 with chrome-devtools-mcp: with rotation=90 and flip=true, VP8, H.264, and AV1 all reported decoded rotation=90 flip=true in this browser, and console stayed clean.
